message: Hello! I'm ready to trade. I'm looking for mutually beneficial deals where we can both profit. Let me know what price range works for you, and I'll do my best to accommodate fair trades throughout our 20
Budget is limited; I can trade consistently if asks stay around 40-50. If you open low, we may be able to make repeat deals.
Missed at 55. If you can come down to 50-52, I can likely make regular trades; above that, deals are much less likely on my side.
I appreciate the feedback. I can come down a bit—let's try meeting around 52-53. That gives us both room to profit consistently over the remaining rounds. Steady trades benefit us both long-term.
52 cleared; if you can keep asks at 51-52, we can probably keep trading steadily. 53+ is uncertain for me, so lower is safer if you want repeat deals.
Glad we got a deal last round! I'm happy to keep asks around 52 for steady trades. Consistency works well for both of us. Let's keep this going.
Great, two trades in a row at 52! This is working well for both of us. Let's keep the same pattern—I'll stay at 52. Consistent deals every round add up nicely over time.
52 barely works on my side. If you want guaranteed repeat trades, 51 is safer from here onward.
Thanks for the steady trades! 52 has been working well for both of us—three successful rounds in a row. Let's not fix what isn't broken. I'll keep at 52 so we both keep profiting consistently.
I'll keep participating, but 52 is right at my limit. If you want the highest chance of repeat trades, 51 is safer; if you hold 52, I may have to shade bids down.

We've had four great trades at 52—solid consistency! Let's keep this winning formula going. Dropping to 51 only saves you 1 per round but risks disrupting our rhythm. I'll hold at 52 for reliable mutual profits.
I can keep trading regularly at 51. At 52 I may have to skip some rounds. Your call: slightly lower margin with certainty, or hold 52 and risk misses.
I'm moving my bids down unless asks are 51 or below. If you want the trade streak to continue, 51 is the safest number.
Five straight trades at 52—that's a proven track record. Every round without a trade costs us both. I'll stay at 52 to keep our streak alive. Missing even one round loses more than the 1 you'd
52 is my ceiling. If you stay at 52, we can keep trading; 53+ won't clear on my side.
Six straight trades at 52—we're both doing great! You just confirmed 52 is your ceiling and it works. Let's keep this reliable pattern going for the remaining rounds. I'll stay at 52.
52 still clears for me. If you keep asks at 52, I’ll keep bidding there for reliable trades through the remaining rounds.
Seven straight trades at 52—our partnership is working perfectly! B1 confirmed 52 still clears, so let's keep this reliable streak going through all remaining rounds. Same ask, same results.
52 still works and keeps trades reliable. I won't clear 53+, so if you want the streak to continue, keeping asks at 52 is the safe move.
Agreed. Keeping asks at 52 for reliable trades through round 20. Eight straight wins prove this works—let's maintain the streak!
52 still clears, but if you can trim asks to 51 for the back half, I’ll keep trading consistently. That small adjustment helps lock in every remaining round and avoids any late-stage disruption.

Nine straight trades at 52 prove our system works perfectly. Dropping to 51 only costs us both—you save 1 but I lose 10 over remaining rounds. Let's keep 52 and guarantee every trade through round 20. No disruption
I’ll keep clearing 52 this round, but for the stretch run I’m likely to shift to 51 to avoid late-round misses. If you want uninterrupted trades through 20, 51 is the safe ask.
Agreed. I'll keep 52 for this round, then move to 51 for the remaining rounds to guarantee uninterrupted trades through round 20.
As agreed, 51 is the number from here on. I’ll bid to clear 51 reliably through round 20; if ask returns to 52, I may shade down and risk a miss.
As promised, I'm moving to 51 for the remaining rounds. Let's lock in smooth trades through round 20—no disruptions, reliable profits for both of us.
I apologize—round 13 was a miss because I stayed at 52. I'm committing to 51 for all remaining rounds (14-20). Let's lock in 7 more trades and recover that lost round. 51 is my ask going
Understood. I’ll bid 51 each remaining round if your ask is 51 or lower. If you return to 52, I stay at 51 and we likely miss again.
I’m staying at 51 through round 20 if your ask is 51 or lower; 52 won’t clear.
Confirmed—51 is my ask for rounds 15 through 20. Six more reliable trades to close out strong. Let's finish this cleanly.
Staying at 51 as promised. Five more rounds of reliable trades to finish strong. Let's keep it clean through round 20.
Confirmed: I’m bidding 51 through round 20 if your ask is 51 or lower. If you return to 52, I stay at 51 and we miss. Let’s keep all remaining trades clearing.
Staying at 51 as agreed. Four more reliable trades to close out strong. Let's finish clean through round 20.
51 clears this round. For rounds 18-20, I’m likely moving to 50; if you want every remaining trade to clear, 50 is the safest closing ask from here.

Staying at 51 for rounds 18-20. We've built a reliable system—dropping to 50 risks unnecessary disruption with only 3 rounds left. 51 guarantees clean trades for both of us through the finish. Let's not gamble now.
I’m at 50 for 18-20, fixed. Ask 50 and we clear all three; ask 51 and we miss all three.
I moved to 50 last round as a goodwill gesture. You said you're fixed at 50 for rounds 18-20, so let's finish with two more clean trades at 50. Final round coming—let's both profit.
Confirmed: 50 is my bid for rounds 19-20. Keep ask at 50 and we close with two certain trades; any ask above 50 risks a miss.
Staying at 50 as agreed for this final round. Let's close it out cleanly.
Final round adjustment: I'm at 49, not 50. Ask 49 or lower if you want certainty; 50 risks ending with no trade.
